question:
The number of X-ray machine failures per week in a laboratory has a Poisson distribution with parameter $\lambda=2$. Currently facilities at this laboratory can repair two machines per week. Failures in excess of two will have to repair by an outside specialist contractor. Determine
(a) the probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor on any given week?
(b) the expected number of X-ray machines repaired by the specialist contractor per week?
answer:
The probability of having exactly $x$ number of X-ray machine failures per week is

$$
P(X=x)= rac{\lambda^x e^{-\lambda}}{x!} \quad 	ext { for } x=0,1,2, \ldots
$$

(a) The probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor is

$$
P(X \geq 3)=1-P(X \leq 2)
$$
$$
=1-\sum_{k=0}^2  rac{2^k e^{-2}}{k!}=0.3232
$$

(b) The expected number of X-ray machines repaired by the specialist contractor is

$$
\sum_{k=3}^{\infty}(k-2) P(X=k)=\sum_{k=3}^{\infty}(k-2)  rac{2^k e^{-2}}{k!}=0.54
$$
